name: Train Sim World® VR: New York

reviews.review_description: I've been playing Train Sim World for years now and love it. So when this went on sale I jumped on it and I have to say it's awesome. It's just very cool having an in person view, like sliding open the side window and looking out of it down the platforms when at a stop. Granted I wish there were more trains (ie. Freight Trains etc...) and routes. But hey for a 2 gb game I'm not that mad about it. Hopefully there will be more to come in the future.
I am originally from NYC and this reminds me of the train so much

The game is good but there is 2 things that have to be add like i thought there was going to be steam trains but when i got it there was only one train it would be cool if there was a steam trains 2 it well be so cool if it was 2 player because on the steam train you can have 2 players doing jobs like to the Coal and one doing  the  watching that's all i want please do that

reviews.review_title: Great for a first VR Train Sim
reviews.review_description:
Man. I feel like people are expecting too much from this honestly. I think we've all gotten so used to VR that we aren't recognizing truly unique and "first in genre" experiences based on these reviews. 

I absolutely love this! Being able to feel like we are driving the train in real life sure is something else. This is the kind of VR experience I've been hoping for since we got this medium. 

London Underground next?

It's good in terms of gameplay mechanics, and the attempt is appreciated, but Quest 3 still lacks the power for a simulator like this. Dovetail's winning move is to port Train Sim World 6 to PSVR2. If Flight Simulator 2024 has been announced for PSVR2, it would be great news if trains were also available, and I'd be willing to pay for them as DLC.

Its a good game but I do feel like the main console and pc games should have a vr mode for psvr2 and pcvr 

Tsw on meta quest is a good game but a vr version of the console version would be better.

The game will never win awards for graphics,but it is enjoyable and time consuming….love the journey sections going from a to b in an allotted time…personally not had hardly any glitches ….few touch ups here and there would be great

The game shows several weaknesses that affect the overall experience. For example, while passengers do board the trains, they simply disappear, which breaks immersion. The stations feel empty and lifeless, which doesn’t help the atmosphere. There is barely any oncoming traffic on the tracks — far too few trains pass by in the opposite direction, making the environment feel very empty. A visual map is completely missing, so you can’t see where you are on the route; instead, only the name of the next station is shown in text. The controls feel imprecise, and there's no haptic feedback, which makes handling more difficult. The graphics are outdated and no longer meet modern standards. Edge smoothing (anti-aliasing) could be improved, especially in the direction of cities, to make visuals sharper. In addition, the tracks don’t render smoothly — they suddenly "pop" into view, which breaks realism. The acceleration of the trains from a standstill takes too long, making the driving feel sluggish. A feature that would greatly enhance the game would be an exterior camera to view the train from the outside. It would also be desirable to ride as a passenger, to experience the journey from inside the train and make the experience more varied and realistic.
Not for everyone, but I enjoy it in bite-sized chunks

this’ a small dev team which
, as far as i can google, is very dedicated. give it some time, friends :) like medieval dynasty and green hell, they’ll showup for all the bugs and most likely for more. i know we train folks are a lot into detail, but time and commitment is too, a very important detail. ;) they’ ll deliver a great game im sure;)

reviews.review_title: Want to become a train driver
reviews.review_description: This game got me into becoming a train driver IRL. The graphics could of look more realistic and It can get boring and tiring an hour during gameplay. Atleast make a animation scene during gameplay where we can see the passengers get on the train that would be cool to watch! The levels are fine, some got me unexpected with speed limit, I did had some game crashes but not as much. Decent game to becoming a train driver yourself!

reviews.review_title: Still can't not play only training track
reviews.review_description: Now that  this has updated I cannot play it on my oculus 2 and it sticks in my s3 it i leave it n come back  anyone else with problem I have remove  n Uninstaller more than twice on both  n no longer can launch from phone

reviews.review_title: I've been workin' on the railroad...
reviews.review_description: Let’s face it; you aren’t playing Train Sim World VR: New York for the visuals, and honestly, for most of the time I was playing I was focused on my tablet screen which shows current speed, speed limit, and any upcoming changes in that limit and the distance to those changes.  There isn’t much time to enjoy the limited and repeating textures whipping by, but there is still plenty of enjoyment and satisfaction to be had if you are into these job simulator type of games.

Ok so I’m a long time Train Sim World player on both Xbox and PC.   Was really looking forward to this.  I know it’s launch day and there will be problems.  Love the first two minutes that I have gotten to play. Game froze up at second stop.   

Also there is no way to adjust height in the game.   I load the intro and my character height is about an inch above the desk. 
 
On the controls page the text is overlapping and none of the buttons do anything on the controls page.   

I want so bad for this game to get batter.  Until then best you can get is a 3 star from me. Hope to see an update today

developer_response.body: Please be advised that as of the latest update, player height is set automatically to give you the best view out the cab. This update also brings many more improvements, so please take a moment to review the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_title: It’s not the Quest. It’s the developers.
reviews.review_description:
What a shame. No one can convince me that the reason this game seems like an Alpha at launch is the Quest performance. Let’s get over the graphics topic that’s normal and it’s not bad. Developers where was your head at? 
- Braking has no sound.
- Between Coast and Forward the sound is always the same and no smooth transition.
- Doesn’t take much to add to the feel of a station and subway, just add button that says “Next stop” and gibberish if it’s too much to do every station (Which is not). Add announcements like “Ding-please stand behind the yellow line blah blah” at the station.
- Open window doesn’t even have any wind sound at full speed.
- Seat height problems and no options to set up and resolve in a game like this ugh.

You know how when you use the emergency brake and (fails to make you feel anything because it doesn’t even have sound effects or anything) you lose points and fail? Well the team who put this together pressed that and failed this project.

It’s embarrassing considering the resources they had like sound effects from being in collaboration or the maker of train sim world franchise. 

I really wanted to like this game but it’s a refund for me especially at this price (Second most expensive game in the store, that’s just funny, because I’ve seen $5 early access games more polished than this) in the next few days if when I try it again nothing has been implemented or no promise of future updates including these additional features.

I’m not paying 30+ dollars for a 3D apartment in New York. Oh and lastly, I think the devs should create an apartment sim VR game where you decorate and clean it. Sure would be good at that since we’ve seen their lack of basic knowledge of making the simplest additions to a train sim game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your detailed feedback. As of the latest update, the game automatically adjusts player height to provide the best possible cab view. This update also features several other improvements—please take a moment to review the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_title: So disappointed after a long wait
reviews.review_description:
So let’s start with some positives. 
Controls are good and immersive  and when approaching stations the VR view really gives the impression of approaching a station. I used to be a tram driver and the control does feel quite realistic.

Height adjustment is a real issue and the first time i loaded in i was literally on the floor under the desk. Had to restart. This desperately needs sorting as i imagine most of us will want to play this seated. 

Graphically the game is so far a little disappointing. Track ahead isn’t always visible and pops into view and the bends are literally straight pieces of track at a different angle. So far i haven’t encountered any other moving trains and the scenery is repetitive and rather basic.  

For me one of the big problems with this title is that most of us are TSW fans who have played on High end consoles and PC’s.  We have waited a long time for this launch and it seems very glitchy. 
Great potential and could be a really impressive title with a little more love and care in the updates. 

I have high hopes for improvements.

developer_response.body: Be advised that as of the latest update, player height is set automatically to give you the best view out the cab. This update also brings many more improvements, so please take a moment to review the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_description:
I’m actually pretty surprised by this game, actually felt like I was driving a train! ( not that I’ve ever driven one) but i felt pretty immersed. 

The controls are great, and my seated position was pretty good however I felt like it needs a seat height position! As I wanted to be a little higher up as it can make your arm go dead sitting lower than the control arm! No dead man’s arm pun intended lol. 

However I overcame  the height issue by using the meta mobility settings and adjusted my height that way and it felt much better, so would be great for the game to add this setting in game. 

The graphics are ok feel they could be tweaked a little more to make the workers and signs approaching you a little clearer and make the scenery a little more pleasing on the eye! 

QGO also sorted these graphical issues for me.

However all said and done I actually love this game really does give you an insight into the concentration needed of a train driver it’s not all throw the lever and sit back!! 

With the above tiny little things implemented it’s definitely a 5 star game from me! 

Great job! Look forwards to completing more train driving challenges.

developer_response.body: As of the latest update, the system now automatically adjusts player height to provide the best possible cab view. This update also features several improvements—please take a moment to review the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_description: This is the very first review I have ever done for any game but felt I needed to write this one. The wait for the game to come out was long and naturally you assume it is because the game is going to be amazing, with great detail adventures. Sadly it has been a huge let down for me, there are several issues with the gameplay itself, lack of detail that could enhance the experience, the inability to adjust the seat so that comfort in control handling is optimized, and the trips themselves are pretty much all the same. There are no real changes throughout the game making things very repetitive throughout the game. The idea is fantastic and it is a great start, but I don’t think it is where is should be after this much time. Hopefully they come out with some updates and fixes sooner than later, otherwise I can’t see people sticking to this game for long.

developer_response.body: Please note that the most recent update now auto-sets player height for the best view. A range of other improvements are included as well, so don’t forget to check out the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_description: Ok. But very glitchy. Height adjustment is an issue and resetting the view doesn't help this. The graphics seem to be like I'm underwater. When in explore mode the walking Is tedious, slow and you can't go in some areas. When taking a photo and developing it and you try to walk at the same time you fly backwards. Graphics in general are very, very basic. Tried to crash the train to see what happens and it seems to be impossible despite going top speed round some really tight corners. Was hoping for alot better than this. Hope there are some updates. I don't understand why developers don't sort these issues out before realising.

developer_response.body: Please note that the most recent update now auto-sets player height for the best cab view A range of other improvements are included as well, so don’t forget to read through the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_description:
I am enjoying this game. Love to blow the horn. The sound effects are spot on.  I feel like I am driving the train. The graphics in the compartment are very good.  I expected a downgrade in graphics  of the tracks and buildings, but not to this level. I was reminded of ps2 console graphics . 

I rated this game a 4 due to the immersion of the game. While I am playing, I am too busy adjusting my speed and blowing the horn at the right time to notice the poor graphics of the buildings.  

I hope the developers will upgrade the graphics, as this is a good purchase now and has the potential to be a great solid game.

As others have said the height issue is a glitch but easily corrected by following the developer’s instructions in other comments. I really don’t see the downside to the building/scenery detail as my focus was on the track ahead and the upcoming speed restrictions and station stops. I don’t see a problem with the switch points not aligning for the intended route. Unless you are changing tracks and risk a derailment this a non-issue for me. Looking forward to future upgrades.

Update: After the recent update, the gloved hands stick to the side window after opening it. They stay there until you exit the game.

reviews.review_title: Better than the trailer makes out
reviews.review_description:
Was a little hesitant whether it would graphically stand up given the limitations of headset capability but pleasantly surprised. Don’t know why the trailer doesn’t show any people at platforms but they do exist (at least on quest 3) and have seen passing trains as well. 

No motion slickness which I was afraid of. controls , sounds work really well .. although do wonder how the train keeps on the decidedly dodgy track.

Shame you can’t stick your head out of the window and see anything, maybe due to limitations but maybe you could do some spooky magic, at least at stations so you can see people getting on and off.

All in all, good fun.

I have played TSW for years on PS4 &PS5, so this is a day one purchase for me. I gave it 5 stars because I want them to do a full on TSW on VR, this is one is really a test of the market. 

This one is very dumbed down and simplistic, but can still give you a taste of operating a real train.. this is great for a casual player to experience and perhaps try the full TSW experience on console or PC. I hope they patch it to fix the issues, and maybe release some DLC later on. Good start Dovetail, now build on it!

Ok so. I was so looking forward to this game and I know it’s day 1 so will have issues but it freezes for a past time and when sitting in the cab I can barely see over the dash and standing I’m touching the roof. There’s no height adjustment in game which is a pain. Considering how good train sim is on the series X and PC I know this game will be good once it’s ironed out so can’t give anymore than 3 stars at present.

developer_response.body: With the latest update, player height is automatically configured to enhance your view from the cab. This update includes various other upgrades—feel free to explore the full list in the release notes: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_description:
Overall I have been enjoying the game but there are quite a few things that should be or need to be changed for me to rate higher than a 3.  I get that this was just released and updates will be coming, but these are the things that I have noticed that are aggravating.

It's difficult to get inside a passenger car to ride. Graphics in drivers compartment are great but the outside environment lacks, a lot. When grabbing the horn handle, hand doesn't "stick" to the handle but floats around it. Camera automatically takes a picture as soon as you take it off your chest. In explore mode walking or teleporting is very slow; waiting on a train to ride takes forever for a train to come to the station; should be able to walk from track to track and not have to teleport; shouldn't be able to walk through or extend hand through objects. No height adjustment. Rail workers just stand around and should be moving, hand waving at least. When in the driver's compartment and you put the window down, outside noise should increase or at least hear the wind blowing. Why are most people bald or wearing a hat? When you're the only person in a station, there is lots of crowd noise which is odd, should have ambient noises of a city.

I've been waiting for this game for a long time and sadly quite disappointed.  I am looking forward to the updates and will update my review then.

developer_response.body: We really appreciate your feedback. Hopefully, you will find good improvements in the latest update. You can check out the release notes here: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/

reviews.review_description: I start up the game and where it says new game or continue it looks like a tv channel from the early 90s where it’s got static waves. It’s just a pain trying to select continue because I can’t even see my hands or selector. So I wave my hand around until continue highlights to select. Please add some kind of customization besides gloves I’d love to be able to customize apparel my train office and home.

developer_response.body: We are sorry to hear that you are having trouble navigating the main menu and would like to gather some more information to share with the dev team. If you could reach out to us with examples and your headset model, it would be much appreciated. You can reach us here: dovetailgames.freshdesk.com/en/support/home

reviews.review_description:
It's not a bad game, but it's New York City, where are the cars driving around people. Some of the stations when I pull in have no body there. Most of the time I'm looking at the tablet, checking my speed and how far the next station is butter have cars driving and people wouldn't hurt. How do you ride the train as a passenger? On explore mode the the train doesn't come and I wait there for at least a few minutes and the one time the train came I was in the white circle by the door and couldn't  get the train is something you have to unlock or something I'm not doing ? I do enjoy this game.               
Update  03/05 .   I saw there was a patch release a couple of days ago, but I had to uninstall and reinstall because the game wouldn't load. Now I'm able to ride the train in explore mode but I had to start from the beginning and I can't get pass level 3 because I have to do the tutorial for the m7 but the folder isn't there and  when it ask to I want to do it I click continue and when I get to the second station it prompt me to leave the cab and i cant.  So, for now, I'm just stuck  on level 2 in career mode and can't advance

developer_response.body: We are truly sorry to hear that you are having trouble accessing the M7 tutorial and would like to help. If you could reach out to our support team with examples, it would be much appreciated: dovetailgames.freshdesk.com/en/support/home

reviews.review_description: My boy loves train sim on xbox. He liked this for 10 mins then got bored. Could have been a great title with addon tracks later down the line but there something missing. The graphics are ok for stand alone.

reviews.review_title: Has potential… But room to improve
reviews.review_description:
As someone who has operated trains in the past this game excited me. And on first play I was very impressed with it. But it’s the details a person like me with real world experience will notice that drag the experience down a little: Simple things like switches. Switch points should be set properly or even move. When I see I’m about to split or pick a switch it’s a little annoying. 

All that said I’m looking g forward to improvements and seeing where this game ends up!

I preordered and really wanted to love this game. Instead, I just like it a little. The graphics have to be the biggest let down. After looking at the PC version of Train Sim 4 and 5, I expected just a slight step-down in quality on the Quest 3. Instead, the environments outside of the train resemble PS2 graphics. Also, not much actual simulation in this sim game. I’m not sure why the developer didn’t try to use the graphic capabilities of the Quest 3.

As someone who has enjoyed Train Sim World in all its iterations right up to Train Sim World 5 using UEVR Injector on PC this standalone version is not a bad effort but could do with some polish. From running TSW 2-5 in VR on my mid range PC i knew it was going to be a struggle to get a game looking even slightly close on Quest. The game on Quest is not the best looking and does not come close to the PC version even on Ultra low settings, no surprise there then. What the standalone version has is it conveys the speed and feels like you are in the cab of a train. As someone who has enjoyed Densha de GO! games over the years, even purchasing the PS4 version from the Japanese PS store so I could play the PSVR mode which was excellent, runs on Unreal Engine 4 like the this game and looks much better, the gameplay of Train Sim World VR brought a smile to my face. It is pretty obvious these devs played Densha at some point, I believe on Switch. The few complaints I have is perhaps they should have played the PS4 PSVR version for research as they may have learnt how to have a hud that is in your view but you don't have to keep looking at. With Train Sim World VR you have a tablet to the left which you constantly have to look at which is not bad but does not feel completely natural, other grumbles are there is no option to turn the rainbow colour Stop Markings off when entering a station, I always switch rainbow Stop Markings off on TSW games, it is the first thing I do. A last grumble is there is no Online Leaderboards when playing the game on Quest 2 so no battling with my kids in competition. After the good and mostly bad feedback from VR YouTubers at launch I done my research and purchased this thinking there was a big possibility I would refund but this did not happen. It will be great as the weather gets better here in the UK and I will be able to play Train Sim World VR in the garden on my portable headset. Hopefully updates can polish it and add a few options.

I’ve been playing this game for 2 days now and it is absolutely fantastic . Been waiting several years for a game like this on VR … and I have finally been rewarded . The realism is awesome and the way you have to remain focused and alert is just like the real deal ! You will love it !  10 out of 10 !

reviews.review_title: Can't finish 3rd mission due to bugs
reviews.review_description:
It could be a nice, relaxing game, but one cannot finish the M7A tutorial as it is impossible to exit the cabin.
Editors:Please fix.
Not playable in this condition.

developer_response.body: Please accept our apologies for the M7A tutorial problem. Rest assured, this was quickly fixed by an update on 16th May 2025. If you experience any further issues, please reach out to us here: https://dovetailgames.freshdesk.com/en/support/tickets/new

reviews.review_description: The update messed up my game. I thought it was my oculus but the screen is messed up like I dropped a tv. Nope fix it back

developer_response.body: We are truly sorry to hear that something is wrong with your game and would encourage you to reinstall it. Should this not fix the problem, please reach out to our support team with more details: https://dovetailgames.freshdesk.com/en/support/home

reviews.review_description: Add rail lights on tracks would be awesome

I like the game but kinda disappointed of The graphics and the track missing pieces.

And I was wondering if y’all can make an nj transit update?

reviews.review_title: train sim world vr missing leaderboard
reviews.review_description:
train sim world vr missing leaderboard
future update add leaderboard
my quest 3
Good controls, awesome visuals. Relaxing gameplay. Worth it.
Definitely going to be Achievement Hunting this one. It does a great job keeping you engaged with the challenges on each level.

reviews.review_title: Nice  but easy height adjustment needed
reviews.review_description:
I pre orderd this game and i like it. Graphics are nice, sounds are nice too.
The seat height is standard too low however,it should be adjustble in-game, that is much more user friendly than fiddling with quests setting. A seat heigh adjustment control option - like derail valley has - could help , and  a speed contol option by thumbstick or buttons, to fine tune the speed in small notches would be nice also. Overall i recommend this game, and i like the relative easy controls in comparison to other train sims, that are overcomplicated. I hope the devs will update soon, for this is a nice and relaxing game to play.

developer_response.body: With the latest update, player height is now set automatically to optimize your view from the cab. This update includes numerous other enhancements, so be sure to check the release notes for full details: https://forums.dovetailgames.com/threads/tsw-vr-new-york-update-1st-may.91208/
